pairs.fixed_distinction: A server is a role that processes requests, while a database is a system that manages data. A database may run as a separate server process or be embedded in an application.
pairs.diagnostic_question: Does an application that reads an SQLite file always need a separate database server?

pairs.fixed_distinction: An API is the broad category of rules for software interaction; MCP is a specific protocol through which an AI host discovers and uses tools, resources, and prompts.
pairs.diagnostic_question: Can an MCP server call an existing GitHub API internally?

pairs.fixed_distinction: API keys and access tokens are access credentials, while an LLM token is a unit of model processing and cost.
pairs.diagnostic_question: Can copying a context token log you in to an API?

pairs.fixed_distinction: The meaning of an API key depends on the provider's contract, while an OAuth access token represents delegated authority with a scope, resource, and duration.
pairs.diagnostic_question: Is every value sent in a Bearer header an OAuth token?

pairs.fixed_distinction: AuthN verifies identity; AuthZ decides whether a specific action on a specific target is allowed.
pairs.diagnostic_question: Can deletion still be denied after a successful login?

pairs.fixed_distinction: Server is a role; local, remote, and cloud describe execution location or operating model.
pairs.diagnostic_question: Is localhost:3000 automatically exposed to the internet?

pairs.fixed_distinction: A folder groups files, a repository is a Git history structure, and a project is a tool-specific scope of work.
pairs.diagnostic_question: Is every project folder a Git repository?

pairs.fixed_distinction: A package is a distributable object; a dependency is a relationship stating that the object is needed.
pairs.diagnostic_question: Can the same package be a dependency of project A but not project B?

pairs.fixed_distinction: A model can select or propose tool use, while the host/application coordinates policy, approval, and invocation. An application/executor actually runs a client-side function tool; an MCP server that receives `tools/call` actually runs an MCP tool.
pairs.diagnostic_question: When tool-call JSON is generated, has the file already changed? Which component actually executes the action?

pairs.fixed_distinction: Configuration is the complete set of settings, an environment variable is one delivery channel, and a secret is a value whose nature requires protection.
pairs.diagnostic_question: Does placing a value in an environment variable automatically make it a secret?

pairs.fixed_distinction: Remote describes a relative location; cloud is a computing-resource delivery model.
pairs.diagnostic_question: Can another Git repository on the same laptop be a remote?

pairs.fixed_distinction: Push transfers Git objects; deploy applies a build, configuration, or release to a target environment.
pairs.diagnostic_question: Does the service change merely because code was pushed to GitHub?
